node.statement: MAT-FINITE-CONGRUENCE-SUM-PSD-001: For finite complex matrices, if O is positive semidefinite in Mathlib's quadratic-form sense and K_i is any finite family of matrices fine->coarse, then the finite sum sum_i K_i^dagger O K_i is positive semidefinite. Moreover, for any finite auxiliary coordinate type aux, the amplified finite sum sum_i (I_aux tensor K_i)^dagger O (I_aux tensor K_i) is positive semidefinite for positive semidefinite O on aux x fine. Lean proves these generic finite matrix theorems in Formalisation/LinearAlgebra/Trace.lean using finite sums of PSD congruences. Julia and WolframScript check representative exact/numerical finite sums and their Gram identities. This is finite-dimensional algebra supporting the local complete-positivity target; it does not assert a sourced physical Kraus representation, categorical channel semantics, trace preservation, unitality, or infinite-dimensional complete positivity.
